title: stachys palustris var. pilosa
intro: Stachys palustris var. pilosa, commonly known as the hairy marsh-wort, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Stachys palustris var. pilosa include reducing inflammation, treating digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
